highlight.title: Netflix's Billion-Dollar Architecture: How Event-Driven Microservices Power Global Streaming
highlight.summary: <cite index='11-21,13-9'>Netflix runs a cloud-native microservices architecture on AWS, supporting hundreds of millions of users across the world with thousands of independent services</cite>. <cite index='18-2,18-3'>By adopting microservices, Netflix handles over 2 billion hours of streaming daily, reducing deployment times from weeks to mere hours</cite>.

resources.title: Microservices Architecture Patterns: A Beginner's Guide
resources.why_now: <cite index='27-15,27-16,27-17'>This guide explains core microservices patterns, service decomposition, and communication styles, written for developers, tech leads, and architects evaluating microservices or planning a migration from a monolith</cite>.

resources.title: Event-Driven Architecture: A Complete Guide (2026)
resources.why_now: <cite index='6-1'>In 2026, EDA is the foundation for microservices, real-time analytics, and AI agent systems, typically built on Apache Kafka, Pulsar, or Redpanda as the event backbone</cite>.

resources.title: CQRS in Action - A Real World Simple Architecture
resources.why_now: <cite index='33-15,33-16'>This real-world example shows how event sourcing and CQRS provide the flexibility to adapt and grow systems without mental tax, addressing the challenge of state-based systems that grow in complexity over time</cite>.

resources.title: 19 Essential Microservices Patterns for System Design Interviews
resources.why_now: <cite index='29-5,29-6'>Microservices have become the go-to architecture for building scalable and resilient applications across industries, and knowing these patterns demonstrates you're up-to-date with industry best practices</cite>.

claude_take: <cite index='2-31'>What's exciting about 2026 is how enterprises are adopting event-driven architecture for microservices interactions at scale</cite>. The patterns that seemed exotic just a few years ago—CQRS, event sourcing, saga patterns—are now proven tools that companies like Netflix use to serve hundreds of millions of users reliably.
